item.title: This Conservative Is Not Afraid to Be a ‘Beautiful Loser’
item.description: What exactly is conservatism in the Trump era?

item.title: What This Pixar Film Gets Wrong About Boys
item.description: Films like “Inside Out” raise questions about how we portray boys’ emotional lives. “It just felt like every time that a male character appeared onscreen in that movie, they were an emotional idiot,” Ruth Whippman says on “The Opinions.”

item.title: With Pitchford v. Cain, The Supreme Court Protected Jury Rights. Now It Must Go Further.
item.description: All Americans benefit from the outcome of this case. And yet the decision highlights just how much work still needs to be done.

item.title: A.I. Money From I.P.O.s Will Change Philanthropy
item.description: Silicon Valley is about to get a lot more liquid. What will it spend its money on?

item.title: The Cruelty of Family Separation
item.description: Readers respond to a news article, “Push to Deport Splits Over 100,000 Families.” Also: The trolling war.

item.title: What Five Years in Big Law at WilmerHale Taught Me
item.description: Despite all our good intentions, the legal system — that thing we were charged to protect — kept getting worse.

item.title: Three Ways Trump Is Losing the War in Iran
item.description: Three reasons Trump is losing the war in Iran.

item.title: We Tried Fuzzy Borders in the 19th Century. It Didn’t Go Well Then, Either.
item.description: A system of fuzzy borders, in which powerful states treat territory as negotiable and sovereignty as conditional, is not a viable alternative to the liberal world order.

item.title: Does Trump Want to Lose the Midterms?
item.description: The president doesn’t seem that concerned that his party could lose control of Congress. Ezra Klein and the Republican strategist Liam Donovan discuss Trump’s midterm strategy and Democratic paths to victory.

item.title: He’s Entitled and Nepotistic. This Is Not What Democrats Need.
item.description: Members of the country’s grandest political dynasty are not inherently qualified to hold office.

item.title: There’s No Escaping the Rot in This Justice Department
item.description: How long can decent people continue to work for such a corrupted institution?

item.title: Pope Leo’s Encyclical on A.I. Is Disappointingly Mild
item.description: “Magnifica Humanitas” was measured and cautious.

item.title: The Doping Olympics Were Underwhelming
item.description: The company in charge of the competition seems to want a world where nobody tells it no.

item.title: The Hard Truth My Party Needs to Face
item.description: We must use our leverage to end Israel’s occupation and establish two states with full political and legal rights for all.

item.title: Hungary Showed How to Defeat an Autocrat
item.description: Eleven lessons America must learn

item.title: Trump Wants to ‘Take Cuba.’ These Cubans Have Thoughts.
item.description: A filmmaker asks residents of the embattled island what they would say to the U.S. president.

item.title: Is the U.S. Prepared for a Major War?
item.description: America has fired “something like eight years’ worth of Tomahawk missile production” in Iran, Christian Brose, the chief strategy officer of Anduril Industries, says on “Interesting Times,” where he and the Opinion columnist Ross Douthat discuss the limitations of America’s arsenal of “luxury” weapons.

item.title: Should We Let Robots Kill People?
item.description: Are we ready for autonomous drones to make life-and-death decisions on the battlefield? Christian Brose, the chief strategy officer of Anduril Industries, explains on “Interesting Times” that the Pentagon’s official policy leaves the door for autonomous weapons wide open.

item.title: America Has a Masculinity Crisis
item.description: A much-needed, nuanced conversation about masculinity and feminism today.

item.title: Does Trump Want to Lose the Midterms?
item.description: The president doesn’t seem that concerned that his party could lose control of Congress. Ezra Klein and the Republican strategist Liam Donovan discuss Trump’s midterm strategy and Democratic paths to victory.

item.title: This Is Why Your Groceries Are So Expensive
item.description: We need to confront America’s highly concentrated food sector, one that has feasted on oligopolistic behavior for more than a century.

item.title: This Is How to Stop Trump’s Vindictive Prosecutions
item.description: We need a law that raises the legal standard for bringing federal criminal charges.

item.title: In a World That Enabled Epstein, What Makes a Good Man?
item.description: A much-needed, nuanced conversation about masculinity and feminism today.

item.title: What Your Kid’s Report Card Isn’t Telling You
item.description: Grades are going up and test scores are going down.

item.title: Commas, Common Sense and Justice
item.description: The ancients developed punctuation to create clarity, the Supreme Court used it to create confusion.

item.title: Israel’s Isolation Is Deepening Fast. It Doesn’t Have to Be This Way.
item.description: Elections this year hold out the promise that the state can change policies in a way to recover some international good will.

item.title: Trump’s Retribution Comes to Texas as Paxton Defeats Cornyn
item.description: Readers discuss Senator John Cornyn’s loss to Ken Paxton in Tuesday’s Republican primary. Also: Investigating E. Jean Carroll; Mets and Knicks; religion and A.I.

item.title: Big Swells of Rancor Are Capsizing America
item.description: Texas just got a lot more interesting.

item.title: Every New Cease-Fire in Lebanon Brings Hope — Until It Doesn’t
item.description: In Lebanon, each new cease-fire is met with blind optimism — as if it hails the end of a conflict instead of what it actually is: an admission ticket to the next war.

item.title: Our Military Is Built for the Wrong Century
item.description: Ukraine and Iran have shown us that war as we’ve known it is over.

item.title: What Cubans Really Think About Trump
item.description: A filmmaker asks residents of the embattled island what they would say to the U.S. president.

item.title: Our Military Is Built for the Wrong Century
item.description: Ukraine and Iran have shown us that war as we’ve known it is over.

item.title: Why the Liberal Narrative Is ‘Collapsing’
item.description: The historian and philosopher Yuval Noah Harari is interested in how stories shape societies. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” he describes the dominant storylines of fascism, Communism and liberalism, and why the liberal narrative is struggling to hold.

item.title: The A.I. Tipping Point?
item.description: A.I. agents are already being used in workplaces. But what happens when they are granted legal personhood? Yuval Noah Harari, the author of “Nexus: A Brief History of Information Networks From the Stone Age to A.I.,” explains on “The Ezra Klein Show.”

item.title: Pope Leo’s A.I. Vision Might Not Be Strange Enough
item.description: He is both a critic and a normalizer of artificial intelligence.

item.title: It’s Crow, Mr. Trump, Not Lobster
item.description: Whatever deal is reached between the United States and Iran, Trump will have to make a big concession: A murderous regime remains intact.

item.title: What Ken Paxton’s Win Means for James Talarico
item.description: The Senate race in Texas will come down to the Democratic candidate’s strengths and the Republican’s weaknesses.

item.title: How Democrats Can Avoid the Mistakes of 2024
item.description: Readers discuss the Democratic Party’s autopsy of the presidential election. Also: Single at a party.

item.title: Time Gave Me Back What Prison Took Away
item.description: A formerly incarcerated writer reflects on how the prison system didn’t foster change, but befriending time did.

item.title: Writing Is Fundamental to How We Think
item.description: A.I. can be a crutch that hurts our ability to think creatively.

item.title: Prison and Time
item.description: A formerly incarcerated writer reflects on how the prison system didn’t foster change, but befriending time did.

item.title: Yuval Noah Harari on Donald Trump’s Core Delusion
item.description: The best-selling author of “Sapiens” and “Nexus” discusses the core delusions deranging our politics.

item.title: Deal or No Deal With Iran
item.description: A deal to end the current blockade is merely an enticement for the next blockade and the one after that.

item.title: Yuval Noah Harari on Donald Trump’s Core Delusion
item.description: The best-selling author of “Sapiens” and “Nexus” discusses the core delusions deranging our politics.

item.title: Outrage Over Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund
item.description: Readers praise the opposition from some Republicans, call the fund a legal “travesty” and discuss the culture of corruption. Also: Living with dementia.

item.title: Progressives Are Listening to the Wrong People on A.I.
item.description: An obsession with the most doomer prospects of A.I. is blinding progressives from thinking about serious, realistic policies.

item.title: Iran Is Trolling Us and We’re Not Doing Anything About It
item.description: This is a novel form of propaganda.

item.title: Forget Ozempic. Brain Health Is the New Wellness Frontier.
item.description: We’ve never been so mindful of our minds

item.title: Falling Test Scores: Reasons and Fixes
item.description: Readers discuss data showing lower reading and math scores. Also: The yuppie factor.
